advantages: Removes duplicated cross-cutting code (logging, timing, retries, caching) from many functions.
Keeps the wrapped function's own logic clean and focused on its core job.
Composable: multiple decorators can be stacked to combine behaviors.
Reusable across any function whose signature the wrapper forwards with *args/**kwargs.
disadvantages: Adds a layer of indirection that can make stack traces and debugging harder to follow.
Easy to break introspection (name, docstring, signature) if you forget functools.wraps.
Stacking order matters and can be confusing — the closest decorator to the function runs innermost.
Hidden behavior at the call site: readers must know the decorator to understand what really happens.
